tool_name: Fired Heater Thermal Efficiency — Stack Loss, Excess Air Optimisation & Process Duty

inputs.name: Fuel Lower Heating Value (LHV)
inputs.note: Natural gas ≈ 47500; refinery fuel gas ≈ 42000–46000; fuel oil ≈ 40000–42000 kJ/kg

inputs.name: Flue Gas O₂ Content at Stack (dry basis)
inputs.note: Measured by portable O₂ analyser at stack; typical target 2–3% for gas fuel

inputs.name: Radiation & Convection Losses (% of fired duty)
inputs.note: API 560: typically 1–2% for well-insulated heaters; up to 3–5% for small units

formulas: XS_air_pct = O2_stack_pct * 100 / (21 - O2_stack_pct)   // [%] Excess Air Percentage from O₂ Stack Reading Reference: API 560 §A.3 — excess air from dry O₂: EA% = O₂% × 100 / (21 − O₂%)

cp_fg_kj_kg_k = 1.004 + 0.000254 * stack_temp_c   // [kJ/(kg·K)] Stoichiometric Flue Gas Specific Heat Approximation (c_p,fg) Reference: ASME PTC 4-2013 — linearised flue gas c_p; accurate to ±2% over 100–600°C

m_air_stoich = 11.5 * fuel_carbon_content_pct / 100; m_fg_kg_kg = m_air_stoich * (1 + XS_air_pct/100) + 1; Q_stack_pct = m_fg_kg_kg * cp_fg_kj_kg_k * (stack_temp_c - ambient_temp_c) / fuel_LHV_kj_kg * 100   // [%] Stack (Dry Flue Gas) Heat Loss Reference: API 560 / ISO 13705:2006 §8.2 — dry flue gas stack loss; m_air stoich from carbon content

eta_th_pct = 100 - Q_stack_pct - radiation_loss_pct   // [%] Net Thermal Efficiency (η_th) Reference: ISO 13705:2006 §8 — efficiency by losses method; unburned hydrocarbon loss neglected for clean fuel

Q_fired_kw = fuel_flow_kg_h / 3600 * fuel_LHV_kj_kg; fuel_utilisation_pct = process_fluid_duty_kw / Q_fired_kw * 100   // [kW, %] Fired Duty & Fuel Utilisation Reference: API 560 §4.3 — fired duty = fuel rate × LHV
fuel_saving_kgh = fuel_flow_kg_h * 0.01; annual_saving_kj = fuel_saving_kgh * 8760 * fuel_LHV_kj_kg / 3600   // [kg/h, GJ/yr] Annual Fuel Saving Potential — 1% Efficiency Improvement

standards: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

V1.error_msg: Stack O₂ < 1%: risk of sub-stoichiometric combustion — CO formation, afterburning, tube coking; increase air supply immediately

V2.error_msg: Stack temperature > 600°C: severe stack heat loss — verify convection section fouling; consider air preheater installation

V3.error_msg: Process duty significantly below thermal efficiency × fired duty: check process-side fouling, flow maldistribution, or tube scaling

smart_warnings.message: Excess air > 25%: significant stack loss penalty — each 10% excess air at 300°C stack increases stack loss ≈ 0.5%; install O₂ trim controller
smart_warnings.source: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

smart_warnings.message: Thermal efficiency < 85%: below API 560 design target for natural gas fuel — assess convection section tube cleaning and air preheater condition
smart_warnings.source: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

smart_warnings.message: Stack ΔT > 350 K: air preheater economiser has strong ROI — each 20°C stack temp reduction raises efficiency ≈ 1%
smart_warnings.source: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

smart_warnings.message: High O₂ (>6%) on large heater: major energy waste — reduce excess air to 2–3% target; NOₓ and stack loss both decrease
smart_warnings.source: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

smart_warnings.message: High radiation loss (>3%): check casing and refractory integrity — hot spots and casing leaks waste energy and create safety hazard
smart_warnings.source: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)
